name: aeon
tagline: Reference skill teaching the aeon Python toolkit's sklearn-style API for time-series classification, forecasting, clustering, and anomaly de
verdict: Clean, safe skill. No credential access, no shell-piped remote code, no manipulative or hidden instructions — the only network action is `uv pip install` of the legitimate, well-known `aeon` PyPI package, version-pinned. Minor quality gaps only: no explicit negative-trigger guidance, no troubleshooting/failure-path section, and a couple of quick-start snippe

highlights.summary: No negative-trigger guidance anywhere (e.g., 'not for non-temporal/tabular data' or 'not for streaming/real-time inference') — a user could misapply it outside its actual fit.

As a library-usage reference skill it never states what deliverable the user should end up with (e.g., saved model, printed metrics, report) — reads as an API cookbook rather than a task-outcome contr

Beyond the general 'Experimental modules' caveat (lines 45-47), there is no troubleshooting section for common failures (install errors, version-mismatch tracebacks, GPU/deep-learning extra-dependency
